Instructions for configuring Apache httpd as a reverse proxy for a WSGI server in a Flask application.

# Purpose
The document provides guidance on configuring the Apache HTTP server (`httpd`) to act as a reverse proxy for a WSGI server. It explains the benefits of using `httpd` to handle incoming requests, TLS, and other security and performance concerns more effectively than a WSGI server alone. The document outlines the basic steps for configuring `httpd`, including modifying the `httpd.conf` file to load necessary modules and set up proxying to a local WSGI server. It also mentions the importance of ensuring that `X-Forwarded` headers are correctly set for the application to recognize forwarded requests. Additionally, it provides instructions for simulating domain name configuration by editing the `hosts` file on Linux systems.
